ab: Adu mahtuik masingav mas su i?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Mahtu, maduazik.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Adu mahtuik matusingav mas su?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Ni, kauman naka hansaipun.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Mais minaunin hai aiza inak ailuan, miliskinik tu adu mahtu kusuan mapasinagav?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Mahtu na ilumahik maltala mas su-u.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Adu aiza su gutunin ailuan asaik masingav su paliuni itu Bunun habasang tu sinihumis.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於布農族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the [Bunun] people.

ab: Kamaninak madia hansaipun haitu na macinanuik tahu inak tu kaihansaipun.
ch: B:我知道的不多，但是我會盡量講我所知道的。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: Ta’aza-iktu kasu hai mahasiapa mas Bunun tu sinlulus-an asa saikin pishaip mas su-u.
ch: A:我聽說您對布農族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Bunun and I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Kauman amin hansaipun haitu mais hasaipun ku hai na altalamunuku.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Adu mahtuik lahaningu su aupa mais habasin hai na ma-aipi iliskinun.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Ung, nitumakua.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Punpia-in isu aipcin hamisan?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Na aizin inak pu masivaun han vau tu hamisan.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: Kasu hai ma’uvazng.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: Ni-i, madaingzin saikin.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: Mais na isa kasu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Mais na sianik Tamazuan.
en: B: I am from [Tamarvan].

ab: Masingavik isutu maluspingaz saia hai Bunun siduh aupa?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Bunun?

ab: Ni, Paiuan saia.
en: B: No, she’s from the Paiwan tribe.

ab: Piasu hansaipun halinga?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Hansiapik malus Bunun sin malus Put.
ch: B: 我會說布農語和閩南語。
en: B: I speak Bunun and Minnan.

ab: Manaskal saikin aip mahtu masingav mas su na pali-unita hahanup sin samu tu hansiap?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Ung, habasang tu mainduduazang malulusbut munludun hanup.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Mihumisang madia isu aipa sinava, tuza tu masanbut.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Mamantuk inak sintahu itu madadaingaz tu hansiap.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Panaupincia imita sinsingav uninang isutu sintahu.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: La’duazavang, namuhnamg ta mapalinutu.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: Namudani saikin na maltala muhnin ta’aza isu tu sintupa tu inihumisan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Ung, kandaukdauka mudadan.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
